items.briefing:
Australian leaders are warning that AI governance is struggling to keep pace with how quickly organisations are adopting AI tools — a concern echoed across the Asia Pacific region, where a skills shortage is compounding the risk. Experts are calling for stronger human oversight of AI systems to prevent unintended or harmful outcomes, and this applies equally to public sector agencies, where AI use is reportedly outpacing readiness.

Separately, deepfake fraud attacks have surged 180%, with identity verification checks increasingly failing to detect them — a growing threat for any organisation that verifies people remotely. **Practical tip:** If your organisation uses video or digital identity checks, ask your provider what specific measures they have in place to detect deepfakes.

featured.title: DigiCert survey finds 81% of Australian organisations hit by AI security incidents or vulnerabilities
featured.quote: 81% of Australian organisations already hit by AI security incidents, showing widespread vulnerability.
